sourceMovie.title: Jarhead
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Jarhead (2005) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and war blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lone Survivor
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jarhead clicked for you, Lone Survivor (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and war, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jarhead clicked for you, Unbroken (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and war, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Jarhead?
faq.answer: Try Lone Survivor, Unbroken, The Thin Red Line—each shares DNA with Jarhead while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Jarhead?
faq.answer: Jarhead (2005) is best described as Drama, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Jarhead part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Jarhead Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
